decisions.body: The decision keeps the project moving by trading speculative flexibility for speed, lower maintenance, and a clearer publishing workflow.
decisions.summary: Keep the portfolio refresh simple by using a static-first deployment with lightweight analytics and no custom backend.
decisions.title: Choose a static-first portfolio stack

decisions.body: This proposed decision gives the starter repo a live example of how unresolved product constraints can still be structured and reviewable.
decisions.summary: Start with local-only storage and revisit optional sync only after the daily habit loop feels dependable.
decisions.title: Keep the habit tracker private-first

decisions.body: This decision keeps the public template focused on onboarding quality and working proof instead of sprawling, half-finished surface area.
decisions.summary: Prove the starter with projects, tasks, and decisions before expanding into tools, contacts, applications, and reviews.
decisions.title: Ship a vertical slice before broader coverage

projects.body: This sample project demonstrates a healthy middle stage: the concept is clear, the core loop is defined, and a few important product decisions are still in motion.
projects.summary: Build a private-first habit tracker with a calm daily check-in and exportable weekly review notes.
projects.title: Local Habit Tracker

projects.body: This meta project keeps the public template honest by focusing on onboarding quality, deterministic tooling, and public-safe sample content instead of private operating detail.
projects.summary: Ship a cloneable PersonalOS starter with sample data, a validation CLI, and deterministic generated outputs.
projects.title: Starter Workspace Launch

projects.body: The project is tightening positioning, simplifying navigation, and packaging a cleaner proof-of-work story for future clients and collaborators.
projects.summary: Refresh the public portfolio so it explains services, proof of work, and writing more clearly.
projects.title: Portfolio Site Refresh

tasks.body: The task is deliberately specific because this starter repo should model how action-oriented tasks connect back to a single parent project.
tasks.summary: Map the daily flow so the habit tracker feels quick, private, and calm from the first session.
tasks.title: Define the v1 habit check-in flow

tasks.body: This blocked task gives the starter a realistic example of how uncertainty and architectural tradeoffs can live safely in a public template.
tasks.summary: Compare local-only storage against optional backup sync so the app stays private without boxing in future needs.
tasks.title: Compare offline sync options

tasks.body: The screenshots turn the portfolio from a text-only refresh into a concrete case study with visible outcomes.
tasks.summary: Export before and after screenshots so the portfolio refresh has immediate visual proof.
tasks.title: Capture case study screenshots

tasks.body: This task is the narrative anchor for the project because it informs the homepage, about page, and top-level call-to-action.
tasks.summary: Write a sharper hero section and service overview for the refreshed portfolio homepage.
tasks.title: Draft homepage positioning copy

tasks.body: This completed task shows how the starter can represent shipped work alongside active and blocked items.
tasks.summary: Add a five-minute quickstart and deterministic tests so new builders can trust the sample workspace.
tasks.title: Publish quickstart and golden tests

workspace.description: Public-safe sample workspace for the PersonalOS template repository.
workspace.name: PersonalOS starter workspace
